evals.prompt: Create a Riverpod provider that fetches products from a repository.
evals.expected_output: Use @riverpod annotation on an AsyncNotifier. The build() method calls the repository. UI uses ref.watch() with AsyncValue.when() for data/loading/error states.

evals.prompt: How do I trigger a navigation side effect when a provider's state changes in Riverpod?
evals.expected_output: Use ref.listen() inside the widget's build() method (ConsumerWidget). Never put side-effects in provider init. Use ref.read() only in callbacks like onPressed.

evals.prompt: How do I override a Riverpod provider in tests?
evals.expected_output: Use ProviderScope with overrides: ProviderScope(overrides: [provider.overrideWithValue(Mock())]). Enable riverpod_lint for cycle detection.

should_not_trigger: How do I create a BLoC for the cart feature?
Set up GetX state management with observables
Add a loading spinner widget to the product list screen
Write a unit test for the repository layer
